metadata.source_title: Made by Marí makes local craft scalable

text:
The crocheted bags, made by Dutch hobbyists, find their way to consumers at home and abroad. Together with the Made by Marí platform, nominated for the ABN AMRO Newcomer Retail Award 2025-2026, the founders also make local craftsmanship scalable for other brands. The story of [Marie Marie Amsterdam](https://marie-amsterdam.com/) – founded in 2022 – starts with a bag that Marthe De Groot buys from her grandmother's crochet club. “I received so many compliments about it at home in Amsterdam,” she says. "Then I thought: what a shame that this kind of craft remains hidden. How can I bring these crocheters together with people who want to buy their work?"
From hobby to community.

metadata.source_title: Made by Marí makes local craft scalable

text:
Together with the Made by Marí platform, nominated for the ABN AMRO Newcomer Retail Award 2025-2026, the founders also make local craftsmanship scalable for other brands. The story of [Marie Marie Amsterdam](https://marie-amsterdam.com/) – founded in 2022 – starts with a bag that Marthe De Groot buys from her grandmother's crochet club. “I received so many compliments about it at home in Amsterdam,” she says. "Then I thought: what a shame that this kind of craft remains hidden. How can I bring these crocheters together with people who want to buy their work?"
From hobby to community. During the corona period, De Groot decides to take the idea seriously.

metadata.source_title: Made by Marí makes local craft scalable

text:
The story of [Marie Marie Amsterdam](https://marie-amsterdam.com/) – founded in 2022 – starts with a bag that Marthe De Groot buys from her grandmother's crochet club. “I received so many compliments about it at home in Amsterdam,” she says. "Then I thought: what a shame that this kind of craft remains hidden. How can I bring these crocheters together with people who want to buy their work?"
From hobby to community. During the corona period, De Groot decides to take the idea seriously. She places calls in Facebook groups and dozens of crocheters sign up in no time.

metadata.source_title: Made by Marí makes local craft scalable

text:
"Then I thought: what a shame that this kind of craft remains hidden. How can I bring these crocheters together with people who want to buy their work?"
From hobby to community. During the corona period, De Groot decides to take the idea seriously. She places calls in Facebook groups and dozens of crocheters sign up in no time. Marie Marie Amsterdam now has a community of 200 crocheters, spread across hubs in the country.

metadata.source_title: Made by Marí makes local craft scalable

text:
How can I bring these crocheters together with people who want to buy their work?"
From hobby to community. During the corona period, De Groot decides to take the idea seriously. She places calls in Facebook groups and dozens of crocheters sign up in no time. Marie Marie Amsterdam now has a community of 200 crocheters, spread across hubs in the country. Each hub has a coordinator who distributes the materials and patterns. “For many women it is valuable to connect their creativity to something bigger,” says De Groot.

Each hub has a coordinator who distributes the materials and patterns. “For many women it is valuable to connect their creativity to something bigger,” says De Groot. "It shows that their handiwork has an impact, far beyond the hobby.

metadata.source_title: Made by Marí makes local craft scalable

text:
"The most difficult thing was to arrange everything operationally and make it scalable. I was in 60 WhatsApp groups and was involved in community management full-time with my mother.". At that moment Rinke Adema comes into the picture. With her experience at tech companies and startups, she immediately sees an opportunity. “I met Marthe and thought: you have to digitize this. Making craftsmanship scalable.

metadata.source_title: Made by Marí makes local craft scalable

text:
I was in 60 WhatsApp groups and was involved in community management full-time with my mother.". At that moment Rinke Adema comes into the picture. With her experience at tech companies and startups, she immediately sees an opportunity. “I met Marthe and thought: you have to digitize this. With a platform that automates everything: from onboarding of makers to project management and payments.”
Making craftsmanship scalable. The two soon decided to think bigger with their platform than just crocheted bags. “It has actually escalated a bit,” Adema laughs.

metadata.source_title: Made by Marí makes local craft scalable

text:
At that moment Rinke Adema comes into the picture. With her experience at tech companies and startups, she immediately sees an opportunity. “I met Marthe and thought: you have to digitize this. With a platform that automates everything: from onboarding of makers to project management and payments.”
Making craftsmanship scalable. The two soon decided to think bigger with their platform than just crocheted bags. “It has actually escalated a bit,” Adema laughs. "We saw that many makers have multiple hobbies: not only crocheting, but also ceramics, making furniture or clothing.

text: Shaking up the industry is desperately needed according to the founders. "Some people find Marie Marie bags expensive. But if you consider how many hours, love and care go into them, it is actually dirt cheap. It really requires a change in consumer mentality. We believe in inspiring, rather than imposing." According to Adema, that change starts with the big brands. “If the brands that made fast fashion big now start recording small-scale, local collections, this will set in motion a huge movement.”. > “We believe this platform can shake up the fashion industry and inspire it to rethink production models.”.

For De Groot, the nomination for the ABN AMRO Newcomer Retail Award 2025–2026 feels like confirmation that they are on the right track with Marie Marie Amsterdam and the Made by Marí platform. “We haven't been around for very long, so it's special that we're being seen by people in the field.” Adema adds: “It is also the perfect stage to get in touch with other retailers who can use our platform.”

metadata.source_title: Made by Marí makes local craft scalable

text:
Founder Made by Marí. For De Groot, the nomination for the ABN AMRO Newcomer Retail Award 2025–2026 feels like confirmation that they are on the right track with Marie Marie Amsterdam and the Made by Marí platform. “We haven't been around for very long, so it's special that we're being seen by people in the field.” Adema adds: “It is also the perfect stage to get in touch with other retailers who can use our platform.”
The nicest and the toughest test. Finally, what do they want to pass on to other starting entrepreneurs? Adema: "Don't give up. You will always encounter hassle and setbacks, but perseverance wins.
